info.title: KTH Action Recognition Dataset
info.subtitle: Recognition of human actions
info.description:
> **⚠️ MIRROR NOTICE:**
> This dataset is a mirror of the official **[KTH Action Database](https://www.csc.kth.se/cvap/actions/)**.
> It is provided here for accessibility and non-commercial research benchmarks. All credits and rights belong to the original authors at KTH Royal Institute of Technology.

# Recognition of Human Actions (KTH Dataset)

This dataset contains six types of human actions (walking, jogging, running, boxing, hand waving and hand clapping) performed several times by 25 subjects in four different scenarios.

### Citation
If you use this database, please cite the following paper:
```bibtex
@inproceedings{schuldt2004recognizing,
  title={Recognizing human actions: a local SVM approach},
  author={Schuldt, Christian and Laptev, Ivan and Caputo, Barbara},
  booktitle={Proceedings of the 17th International Conference on Pattern Recognition, 2004. ICPR 2004.},
  volume={3},
  pages={32--36},
  year={2004},
  organization={IEEE},
  doi={10.1109/ICPR.2004.1334462}
}
```

---

### Dataset Specifications

| Metric | Details |
| --- | --- |
| **Total Sequences** | 2,391 |
| **Classes** | 6 (Walking, Jogging, Running, Boxing, Hand waving, Hand clapping) |
| **Subjects** | 25 persons |
| **Scenarios** | 4 (Outdoors, Scale variation, Different clothes, Indoors) |
| **Resolution** | 160x120 pixels |
| **Frame Rate** | 25 fps |
| **Avg. Length** | 4 seconds |
| **Background** | Homogeneous |
| **Camera** | Static |

### Experimental Setup (Official Split)

According to the original paper (Schuldt et al., 2004), the data is typically split based on subjects to ensure generalization:

* **Training Set (8 subjects):** Persons 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18
* **Validation Set (8 subjects):** Persons 19, 20, 21, 23, 24, 25, 01, 04
* **Test Set (9 subjects):** Persons 02, 03, 05, 06, 07, 08, 09, 10, 22

### Scenarios

The actions are performed in four different settings to test robustness:

1. **s1:** Outdoors
2. **s2:** Outdoors with scale variation
3. **s3:** Outdoors with different clothes
4. **s4:** Indoors

### Data Structure

The dataset contains **25 x 6 x 4 = 600** video files.

* **Original Format:** AVI (DivX-compressed)
* **Filename Convention:** `person<ID>_<action>_<scenario>_uncomp.avi`
* *Example:* `person15_walking_d1_uncomp.avi`
